headlines.text: US and Iran negotiators reach 60-day ceasefire MoU — Trump's approval still pending
Israel strikes Tyre, Lebanon with waves of airstrikes killing at least 14; orders evacuation of 14% of country
Iran attacks Kuwait air defenses in 'egregious ceasefire violation,' CENTCOM says
Trump refiles $10 billion defamation lawsuit against The Wall Street Journal
Ken Paxton defeats Sen. John Cornyn in Texas Senate primary runoff; judge declines to block Trump mail-in voting order
S&P 500 and Nasdaq hit fresh all-time records as Iran deal rumors lift markets; PCE inflation comes in at 3.8%
National gas average falls 12 cents to $4.42 as Iran peace talk optimism cools oil prices
60 Minutes correspondent Sharyn Alfonsi out at CBS after Bari Weiss-led overhaul; follows Anderson Cooper exit
The Mandalorian and Grogu opens to $98M domestically over Memorial Day — solid but lowest Star Wars debut ever
CIA official David Rush charged with stealing tens of millions in gold and foreign currency for 'work expenses'

breaking.text: BREAKING: White House confirms US-Iran negotiators have agreed to MoU for 60-day ceasefire; awaiting Trump's final approval

stories.hed: Negotiators Agree on 60-Day Iran Ceasefire MoU — Ball Is in Trump's Court
stories.dek: US and Iranian negotiators have hammered out a memorandum of understanding for a 60-day ceasefire extension and a phased Strait of Hormuz reopening, but it still needs Trump's signature — which, given that he also threatened to 'blow up' Oman this week, is not guaranteed. Iran would pledge no nukes, clear mines in 30 days, and maybe get some frozen assets back, while Trump decides whether this counts as winning.

stories.hed: Israel Orders Evacuation of 14% of Lebanon as Tyre Strikes Kill 14
stories.dek: Israel struck Tyre with waves of airstrikes Thursday, killing at least 14 people and claiming the targets were Hezbollah infrastructure. Aid agencies are warning of an 'absolute catastrophe' as shelters in Sidon hit full capacity and residents are told to flee north to Beirut.

stories.hed: Ken Paxton Beats Cornyn in Texas, Judge Lets Trump Mail-In Voting Order Stand
stories.dek: Trump-backed Ken Paxton crushed incumbent Sen. John Cornyn by more than 25 points in Tuesday's Texas Senate primary runoff, completing a costly MAGA takeover of the Texas GOP. Separately, a federal judge Thursday declined to temporarily block Trump's executive order restricting mail-in voting, keeping it in effect pending further legal challenges.

stories.hed: CBS Fires 60 Minutes' Sharyn Alfonsi After Bari Weiss Censorship Dispute
stories.dek: Veteran 60 Minutes correspondent Sharyn Alfonsi is out at CBS after news chief Bari Weiss declined to renew her contract, six months after Weiss spiked Alfonsi's segment on Trump's use of El Salvador's CECOT prison. Alfonsi's parting shot: do not expect the network to tell the truth about why she's gone.

stories.hed: Trump Threatened to Blow Up Oman — and the Markets Barely Flinched
stories.dek: The President of the United States told reporters this week that Oman would 'behave or we'll have to blow them up,' regarding a Strait of Hormuz toll proposal — and Wall Street responded by setting new record highs. Whether this reflects supreme market confidence or a complete dissociation from geopolitical reality is, at this point, unclear.

markets.context: Stocks hit fresh all-time highs after Axios reported a US-Iran ceasefire MoU, though oil also spiked on renewed IRGC strikes in Kuwait — proving markets can be bullish and confused simultaneously.

gas.note: Down 12 cents from last week — Iran peace talks are doing more for your wallet than any president ever promised.

popCulture.hed: The Mandalorian and Grogu Debuts at $98M Domestic Over Memorial Day — Baby Yoda Still Has the Force, Barely
popCulture.dek: Star Wars returned to theaters for the first time in seven years and opened to $98 million domestically and $167 million worldwide over the Memorial Day four-day frame — enough to top the charts but technically the lowest domestic opening in Star Wars live-action history. The A-minus CinemaScore and solid word-of-mouth suggest Grogu's grip on audiences remains strong even if the box office isn't at Skywalker levels.

genZ.definition: A viral TikTok audio format revived by The Devil Wears Prada 2's release, where creators deploy Miranda Priestly's icy dismissal line to shut down anyone overexplaining, oversharing, or simply existing too loudly in their presence. It exploded across FYPs in May 2026 as the sequel dropped and has become the go-to sound for vibe-contrast lip syncs and workplace passive aggression content.
genZ.usage: Trump reading the Iran MoU term sheet, sliding it back across the table: 'That's all.'

tldr: US and Iranian negotiators agreed on a 60-day ceasefire framework that could reopen the Strait of Hormuz, but Trump has not yet signed off — meaning the war that started 89 days ago is somewhere between almost-over and absolutely-not-over. Meanwhile, stocks hit records, gas prices dipped, Baby Yoda made $98 million, and a CIA guy apparently kept tens of millions in gold at his house for 'work.'
